metadata.title:
BIG INDUSTRIAL
OUTLOOK FOR


ROSS PARK

pages.raw_text:
1t Is not considered likely that
there will be a very energetic
campaign made by the residents
of Ross Park against the building
by the Washington Water Power
Co. of subsidiary plant in that
locality. 1t is being generally &C
cepted by property holders that
in time their ground will be more
valuable for industrial purposes
than as residence property. From
present indications an immense in
dustrial district will be built along
the river and the line of the Spo-
kane Falls & Northern railway,
and thousands will be employed in
the mills and factories.


1t is reported the Washington
Mill Co will enlarge its plant, and
the King Sash, Door & Lumber
Co. will erect large factory on
the railway line. The payroll in
the industrial plants in operation
in that locality now is estimated
at $1,000 per day, and it is claimed
that this sum will be doubled dur
ing the next few months.
